skill.name: digital-twin-discharge-drafter
skill.description: Generates AI-enhanced patient discharge summaries using digital twin models to predict post-discharge outcomes and optimize care transitions. Produces clinical summaries, personalized patient instructions, and risk-stratified care plans.
skill.summary: AI-powered discharge documentation with digital twin predictions for reducing hospital readmissions
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 52 potential issues across 7 files (990 lines). All findings were evaluated and confirmed as false positives: 36 detections were markdown code block backticks misidentified as shell execution backticks, 5 were innocent text triggering cryptographic/reconnaissance pattern matches, 1 was a legitimate source repository URL flagged as a hardcoded URL, and the critical heuristic was an aggregation of false-positive signals. The actual Python code in scripts/main.py is clean with no network calls, shell execution, credential handling, or data exfiltration. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 36 occurrences of backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are all standard markdown code fence delimiters (```) used to format code examples in documentation, not executable shell commands. The skill uses ```bash, ```python, and ```text code blocks for illustrating API usage.

low_findings.title: Legitimate source URL flagged as hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Line 7 of SKILL.md contains a hyperlink to the skill's own GitHub source repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a standard documentation reference, not a hardcoded credential or exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: Innocuous text misidentified as security patterns
low_findings.description: Multiple lines in SKILL.md and scripts/main.py were flagged for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' based on pattern matches against words like 'digital', 'hash', or 'execution'. These are ordinary documentation and code text with no security relevance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown code fence false positive pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill's SKILL.md contains extensive markdown code blocks using triple-backtick fences (```). The static analyzer misidentified these as shell command-execution backticks. This is a known false-positive pattern for skills with rich documentation containing code examples. The pattern triggered 36 of 52 total findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Keyword-based false positive on 'digital' in digital twin
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged occurrences of the word 'digital' as potential weak cryptographic algorithm references (e.g., MD5, SHA1 digital signatures). The term 'digital twin' is a medical simulation concept and has no relation to cryptography.

content.value_statement: Manual discharge documentation is time-consuming and prone to omissions that increase hospital readmission rates. This skill uses digital twin patient models to generate AI-enhanced discharge summaries, personalized care instructions, and risk-stratified follow-up plans that improve care transitions.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured discharge summaries with admission reason, hospital course, diagnoses, and medications
Calculates readmission risk scores using digital twin model parameters
Simulates post-discharge outcomes across multiple adherence scenarios (optimal, non-adherent, missed follow-up)
Creates personalized patient instructions adjusted for health literacy level, language, and cultural factors
Produces risk-stratified care plans with intervention recommendations based on patient risk scores
Validates discharge summary completeness against standard quality checks
limitations: Digital twin predictions are illustrative examples only and must be validated against real clinical data before clinical use
The skill does not connect to electronic health record systems or external databases
Patient data must be provided as structured input; the skill cannot extract data from medical records or images
All AI-generated clinical content requires physician review and approval before use with real patients

use_cases.title: Hospital discharge workflow automation
use_cases.description: A clinical documentation specialist uses the skill to draft discharge summaries from structured admission and treatment data, reducing documentation time while maintaining completeness standards.

use_cases.title: Readmission risk assessment and care planning
use_cases.description: A care transition coordinator inputs patient risk factors and receives a risk-stratified care plan with recommended interventions, follow-up schedules, and patient education materials.
use_cases.target_user: Care transition coordinators and case managers

use_cases.title: Medical education and simulation training
use_cases.description: A medical educator uses the skill to generate sample discharge summaries from synthetic patient scenarios, teaching residents how to document hospital courses and plan post-discharge care.
use_cases.target_user: Medical educators and residency program directors

prompt_templates.title: Basic discharge summary generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a discharge summary for patient PT12345 admitted on 2026-03-15 with community-acquired pneumonia. Hospital course included IV antibiotics for 3 days, transition to oral antibiotics on day 4, unremarkable recovery. Discharge diagnoses: pneumonia (resolved), hypertension (controlled). Discharge medications: amoxicillin 500mg TID for 7 days, lisinopril 10mg daily. Patient is a 68-year-old male living with spouse. Follow-up with PCP in 7 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Routine hospital discharge with a straightforward clinical course

prompt_templates.title: High-risk discharge with digital twin predictions
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive discharge summary with digital twin predictions for patient PT67890, a 72-year-old female with CHF exacerbation, diabetes type 2, and chronic kidney disease stage 3. Admission 2026-04-01, discharge 2026-04-08. Hospital course: IV diuresis, insulin management, nephrology consult. Lives alone, limited transportation. Include readmission risk simulation for medication-adherent and non-adherent scenarios.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex patient with multiple comorbidities and social risk factors

prompt_templates.title: Patient-friendly discharge instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create patient-friendly discharge instructions for a 55-year-old post-surgical patient (cholecystectomy, uncomplicated). Health literacy level: 8th grade. Language: English. Include medication schedule in table format, warning signs requiring immediate medical attention, activity restrictions for 2 weeks, and follow-up appointment details. Patient has transportation barriers and limited caregiver support.
prompt_templates.scenario: Post-surgical discharge requiring clear patient education materials

prompt_templates.title: Batch processing and risk escalation
prompt_templates.prompt: Process a batch of high-risk patient discharges from the cardiology unit. For each patient, calculate readmission risk using digital twin parameters, generate a risk-stratified care plan, and flag any patient with risk score above 0.30 for automatic escalation to care coordination. Include CHF-specific discharge education and daily weight monitoring instructions for all patients.
prompt_templates.scenario: Unit-wide discharge planning for high-acuity cardiac patients

output_examples.input: Generate discharge summary for CHF patient with readmission risk assessment
output: DISCHARGE SUMMARY - Patient PT67890
Admission Date: 2026-04-01 | Discharge Date: 2026-04-08
Primary Diagnosis: Acute CHF exacerbation
Hospital Course: IV diuresis days 1-4, transitioned to oral diuretics day 5. Nephrology consult day 3. Blood glucose managed with sliding scale insulin. Weight decreased from 82kg to 74kg.
Discharge Medications: Furosemide 40mg BID, Lisinopril 20mg daily, Metformin 500mg BID
Readmission Risk Score: 0.22 (Moderate)
Key Risk Factors: Living alone, transportation barriers, CKD stage 3
Recommended Interventions: Telehealth monitoring, home health visit within 48 hours, pharmacy consultation
Follow-up: Cardiology in 7 days, PCP in 14 days, labs in 5 days

output_examples.input: Simulate post-discharge outcomes for medication-adherent vs non-adherent scenarios
output: OUTCOME SIMULATION - 30-Day Projection for Patient PT67890
Scenario: Medication Adherent - Readmission Risk: 8%, Recovery Time: 14 days, Cost: Baseline
Scenario: Medication Non-Adherent - Readmission Risk: 25%, Recovery Time: 28 days, Cost: +$8,500
Scenario: Missed Follow-up - Readmission Risk: 18%, Recovery Time: 21 days, Cost: +$4,200
Recommendation: Prioritize medication adherence support and confirm follow-up appointments before discharge.

output_examples.input: Create patient-friendly discharge instructions at 8th grade reading level
output: YOUR DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS - Please read carefully
Why you were in the hospital: You had fluid build-up around your lungs because your heart was not pumping as well as it should.
Your medicines: Take furosemide 40mg twice a day (breakfast and dinner) to remove extra fluid. Take lisinopril 20mg once a day for your blood pressure.
Warning signs - Call your doctor right away if: You gain more than 3 pounds in one day, you have trouble breathing when lying flat, you feel dizzy or confused, or you have chest pain.
Activity: Walk slowly for 10 minutes twice a day. No heavy lifting for 2 weeks.
Your appointments: Heart doctor on April 15 at 10:00 AM. Regular doctor on April 22 at 2:00 PM.
If you need a ride to your appointments, call our transportation help line at the number on your card.

best_practices: Always provide both clinical and patient-friendly versions of the discharge summary to support provider documentation and patient understanding
Validate medication reconciliation by comparing admission and discharge medication lists before finalizing the summary
Document the digital twin model version and prediction confidence levels used in risk calculations for audit trail purposes
anti_patterns: Do not use AI-generated discharge content with real patients without a licensed physician reviewing and approving every section
Do not skip the scope validation step — if patient data is incomplete or outside the documented workflow, request missing inputs instead of fabricating clinical details
Do not present digital twin risk predictions as definitive medical prognoses; they are statistical estimates meant to inform, not replace, clinical judgment

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: It generates structured discharge summaries, calculates readmission risk scores, simulates post-discharge outcomes, creates patient-friendly instructions, and produces risk-stratified care plans using digital twin patient models.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to hospital EHR systems?
faq.answer: No. The skill works with structured data you provide as input. It does not connect to electronic health record systems, databases, or any external APIs.

faq.question: Can I use the output directly with real patients?
faq.answer: No. All AI-generated clinical content must be reviewed and approved by a licensed physician before use with real patients. The skill produces drafts and decision-support materials, not final medical documents.

faq.question: What is a digital twin in this context?
faq.answer: A digital twin is a computational model representing a patient's health status, risk factors, and predicted outcomes. In this skill, it is a data structure with parameters like readmission risk base rate, not a real-time simulation connected to patient monitoring.

faq.question: What input data does the skill require?
faq.answer: The skill needs patient demographics, admission details, hospital course summary, discharge diagnoses, and medication lists. Digital twin model parameters and risk factors are optional inputs that enable predictive features.

faq.question: What languages does the patient instruction feature support?
faq.answer: The skill framework supports multi-language patient instructions through its language_preference parameter, including English and Spanish. Translation quality depends on the underlying AI model and should be verified by a bilingual reviewer for clinical accuracy.
